reasoning: Initial observation shows d1=10, d2=20, d3=30 and total_distance=60. The sum of the segments (10+20+30) equals the total distance. To verify this relationship holds when variables change, I will increase d1 by 1 unit and observe if total_distance increases by 1.

The initial observation showed d1=10, d2=20, d3=30, and total_distance=60. After increasing d1 by 10 units (from 10 to 20), the total_distance increased by exactly 10 units (from 60 to 70). This strongly suggests a linear relationship where total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. To further verify this, I will increase d2 by 5 units and check if total_distance increases by exactly 5 units.

The previous steps showed that increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. This confirms total_distance = d1 + d2 + constant. Since d3 is currently 30 and total_distance is 80, and d1+d2=50, the constant appears to be 30, which equals d3. To rigorously confirm that total_distance is exactly the sum of d1, d2, and d3, I will decrease d3 by 5 units and verify that total_distance decreases by exactly 5 units.

The observations consistently show that total_distance equals the sum of d1, d2, and d3. Increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. Increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. Decreasing d3 by 10 decreased total_distance by 10. The relationship total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3 is strongly supported by all data points collected.
